title: Image → document + linked card
description: Read each uploaded image, file what it says as a searchable document, and add a context card pointing back at that document.
hint: Drop images into uploads/, then pick the collection in config.

do.name: note
do.prompt: Look at the image {image} and write a markdown note capturing what it actually says. Transcribe any text, labels, axis titles, table cells and callouts verbatim — this note is the ONLY thing that will be searchable later, because the image itself cannot be. Describe the structure (what kind of chart/diagram/screenshot it is, what it is comparing) and state plainly what a reader should take away. Do not speculate beyond what is visible; if part of the image is unreadable, say so.

do.name: index
do.prompt: Read notes/{image}.md. Pick the ONE term a colleague would type when looking for this — a part number, tool id, step name, or the subject's proper name — and write a two-sentence summary of the note. Reply with a JSON object holding: term (that lookup term), title (a short display name), summary (the two sentences), and source (set it to exactly notes/{image}.md).

steps.title: File these images into the collection?
